item.title: War empties Dubai’s malls and rattles struggling luxury industry
item.description: Footfall and sales drop sharply in emirate’s shopping centres as companies battle supply chain chaos

item.title: Italian gunmaker Beretta pushes to raise its stake in Sturm Ruger
item.description: Rifle-maker has dismissed pressure campaign as an ‘ongoing creeping takeover’ as stand-off escalates

item.title: Hedge fund Caxton extends losses to $1.3bn as Iran war rocks markets
item.description: London-based firm has become high-profile victim of market upheaval sparked by Middle East conflict

item.title: African economies brace for fuel shortages
item.description: Some countries across the continent are imposing fines on people hoarding petrol, or subsidising fuel prices

item.title: Reeves is right to resist universal help on energy bills
item.description: UK’s fiscal constraints leave government reliant on blunter, supplier-side support

item.title: Pop Mart shares tumble over reliance on Labubu
item.description: Chinese maker of global hit doll is world’s most valuable toy company

item.title: Que Sora, Sora
item.description: RIP Sora. OpenAI video generation platform, 2024-26. We hardly knew ye.

item.title: What Americans moving to the UK need to know about tax
item.description: To make sure you avoid the traps, time your arrival and restructure your trusts

item.title: What’s moving long-term interest rates?
item.description: Plus, US bank regulations

item.title: A suitor eyes Jefferies
item.description: Japan’s Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group is working on plans for a possible takeover of the Wall Street investment bank

item.title: Italy’s Meloni looks for gas supplies in Algeria
item.description: Prime minister struggles to find alternatives to Qatari LNG shipments curtailed by Iran war

item.title: Estée Lauder — Puig mix-and-match would be hard to absorb
item.description: Investor disapproval of possible tie-up reflects the challenge of integrating two different family controlled companies

item.title: European airlines hold off jet fuel hedging in Iran war gamble
item.description: Traders expect prices to fall later this year, giving carriers hope they can lock in lower rates by being patient

item.title: Oil and gas companies seize the chance to lobby for Arctic drilling
item.description: Energy security arguments being pushed in response to review of EU policy

item.title: Germany’s Haub family revives $2bn listing plans for OBI and Kik retail businesses
item.description: Investment group Tengelmann working on a proposal that could lead to a spin-off as soon as this year

item.title: Europe needs more private credit, not less
item.description: While problems with the financing source are emerging, the region needs to diversify from bank funding

item.title: Farage’s Reform UK drops pledge to nationalise water and energy companies
item.description: Rightwing populist party is shifting back to ‘small state’ economic policies

item.title: BNP Paribas muscles in on the Japanese buyout market
item.description: European banks grab 10% share in burgeoning market for financing private equity deals

item.title: The Infinity Machine — a deep dive into the mind of Demis Hassabis
item.description: Sebastian Mallaby’s compelling biography of the DeepMind co-founder traces his life’s mission to create artificial general intelligence

item.title: How the shadow fleet is capitalising on the chaos of war
item.description: The Iran conflict has offered the illicit oil trade a lifeline — while the easing of US sanctions widens a transatlantic rift

item.title: Hot springs to rice crackers: Japan’s small businesses suffer Iran war energy crunch
item.description: Reliance on imported fuel is strangling the world’s fifth-largest economy

item.title: China reviews $2bn Manus sale to Meta as founders barred from leaving country
item.description: Deal scrutiny deepens over official fears of strategic tech flowing overseas

item.title: Private credit’s public reckoning
item.description: Is private credit at an inflection point?

item.title: Musk lawyers try to bar Delaware judge over LinkedIn ‘like’ cheering legal defeat
item.description: Quinn Emanuel says judge must recuse herself over post, which judge says she may have liked ‘accidentally’

item.title: Canada and Norway move to capitalise on Iran war oil price surge
item.description: Nations position themselves as reliable suppliers in a ‘desperate’ world

item.title: Merck nears $6bn biotech deal to boost cancer drug pipeline
item.description: Terns Pharma stock has jumped since promising clinical data on its bone and blood cancer treatment last year

item.title: Pentagon appears to be ‘punishing’ Anthropic in violation of free speech, judge says
item.description: Jurist expresses scepticism about Trump administration’s position and calls defence department’s actions ‘troubling’

item.title: Investors snap up debt to finance Electronic Arts’ $55bn take-private
item.description: Bond deals tied to acquisition of video game maker show appetite for risky debt despite geopolitical tumult

item.title: OpenAI to end Disney deal and Sora video app
item.description: CEO Sam Altman is shifting AI start-up’s strategy to focus on its core products

item.title: Charting the OpenAI ‘ecosystem’
item.description: Map of the problematique
